candidate_grammar.boundary: All propositions and finite proofs generated from canonical forms, two held orientations, joint and alternative constructions, registered inference transitions and complete dependency traces.
candidate_grammar.generator: Generate the complete product of proposition identity, denial, conjunction, disjunction, implication, inference provenance, consistency, soundness, grammar completeness and extra-logical status.

excluded_inputs: no global completeness beyond a generated grammar
intended_certificate: Independent regeneration of the complete 1024-candidate product, its sole all-preserving survivor, closure properties and controls.
statement: An admitted proposition is a canonical distinguished form with a held orientation; denial is the retained complementary orientation, not a negative number. Conjunction retains joint proofs, disjunction retains a chosen generated branch and its alternatives, implication is a registered premise-to-conclusion transition, and a proof is a complete dependency trace of accepted rules. Consistency, soundness and completeness are machine-checkable only relative to the exact registered finite grammar and rule set.
title: Exact finite logic, inference and proof boundaries
